etymology.description: The word 一切 (yīqiè, "everything") is composed of two characters: 一(一) (yī, "one") and 切(切) (qiè, "all; each").
etymology.interpretation: The character 一(一) (yī, "one") signifies singularity and entirety, suggesting totality when combined with 切(切) (qiè, "all; each"), which extends the concept to mean 'collectively' or 'every single one.' Together, 一切 (yīqiè) embodies the idea of 'everything' by emphasizing the inclusion of all possible parts or elements as a single unified whole.

examples.chinese: 一切都会好起来(一切都會好起來)。
我们需要为一切做好准备(我們需要為一切做好準備)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen xūyào wèi yīqiè zuò hǎo zhǔnbèi.
examples.english: We need to prepare for everything.

examples.chinese: 他失去了对一切的信心(他失去了對一切的信心)。
examples.pinyin: Tā shīqùle duì yīqiè de xìnxīn.
